dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8862 IH: No Special Immunity for Violating Our State Laws Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Scanlon (for herself, Ms. Tlaib, and Mr. Johnson of Georgia) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend title 18, United States Code, to waive immunity for certain acts committed by Federal law enforcement officers.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the No Special Immunity for Violating Our State Laws Act of 2026.

section.header: Waiver of immunity for certain acts committed by Federal law enforcement officers

subsection.text: Chapter 211 of title 18, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

section.header: Jurisdiction of State over offenses committed by Federal law enforcement officers
section.text: Any person who, while engaged in the performance of duties pertaining to the enforcement of the immigration laws (as defined in section 101 of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1101)), commits any act that causes the death of, or serious bodily injury to, any other person, is not immune from prosecution under the law of a State.

subsection.text: The table of sections for chapter 211 of title 18, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

toc.toc-entry: 3245. Jurisdiction of State over offenses committed by Federal law enforcement officers.
